summary: FL RON Compliance M4 (journal logging, CSV export, admin compliance dashboard, subpoena workflow) end-to-end verified. Backend: 27/27 pytest pass on /app/backend/tests/test_fl_journal_m4.py covering POST/GET/list/get-one/export.csv for journal, admin overview + ceremonies, subpoena intake/list/get/export/respond, plus auth (401), role (403 for demo, 403 for notary on admin endpoints) and validation (400 invalid act type, 422 missing intake fields). Frontend (token-injection auth bypass to validate pages because UI form login redirect wasn't observed within wait window): /notary/fl-journal renders fl-journal-page + new-entry-btn + export-csv-btn + entry-count + filter-act-type/start/end + refresh-btn, and the TEST entry we created via backend appears (TEST Quitclaim Deed, Acknowledgment, $25.00). /admin/fl-compliance renders all 8 KPI cards (kpi-journal=2, kpi-ceremonies=6, kpi-kba 0%, kpi-av 66.7%, kpi-notaries=2, kpi-retention 0/2, kpi-subpoenas open 0 / 2 lifetime, kpi-generated) + fl-ceremonies-table with 6 rows (each row chips J/K/AV/L for gates) + refresh-overview-btn + goto-subpoena-btn. /admin/subpoena renders admin-subpoena-page, subpoena-list with 2 rows (TEST-CASE-M4-001 + FL-2026-001, both RESPONDED), clicking a row opens subpoena-detail-modal with audit-trail (INTAKE/EXPORTED/RESPONDED), export-bundle-btn, and response-record. Ceremony seal auto-log block exists in ceremony_routes.py with idempotency guard.

integration_issues.issue: Email/password Sign In via the rendered form did not redirect to dashboard within 3.5s wait window in this run (URL stayed at /login). The backend /api/auth/login returns access_token correctly (also surfaces requires_2fa + temp_token fields), so the page may be waiting on 2FA gate or a redirect handler. We bypassed by injecting the token into localStorage and pages then rendered correctly. Worth a quick check that LoginPage handles the response shape {access_token, requires_2fa:false, temp_token:null} when 2FA is not enabled.

issues: data-testid 'submit-response-btn' only renders when subpoena.status != 'responded' (verified: count=0 when status='responded', response-record block renders instead). This is correct behavior but the spec wording 'Mark responded transitions status' should note the button is only shown for non-responded subpoenas.

action_items: (OPTIONAL) Investigate why LoginPage form submit didn't redirect in this run — possibly a guard on requires_2fa=false flow, or react-router redirect timing. Direct token injection works, so the bug (if any) is purely UX.

(NICE-TO-HAVE) Add a state-aware empty-state hint when a notary visits /notary/fl-journal with zero entries — currently no-entries testid present but only renders when entries.length===0 (verified working: hidden when 1 entry rendered).
critical_code_review_comments: fl_journal_routes.py:259-263 has a dead-code block — `fl_notaries = await db.fl_notary_credentials.count_documents({}) if hasattr(db, 'fl_notary_credentials') else 0` followed by another try/except that overwrites it. Remove the first line; the try/except below is sufficient.

fl_journal_routes.py:307 — admin/compliance/ceremonies uses `$or: [{ceremony_id: cer_id}, {completed_at: {$gte: j.created_at}}]` to consider a KBA pass as satisfying the gate; this is forgiving and will mark the gate true for any KBA passed AFTER the jurisdiction record. Make sure this matches the readiness endpoint's stricter check or document the divergence.

fl_journal_routes.py export.csv endpoints stream a single io.StringIO buffer via iter([body]) — for very large exports (10y retention worth of entries) consider true async streaming to avoid loading the full result set into memory.
Subpoena export.csv mutates audit_log on every call — exporting twice produces two 'exported' entries; that's intentional and correct for chain-of-custody but document it.

All /api/fl/journal/* and /api/fl/admin/compliance/* helpers re-implement `_get_user` / `_require_admin` already present elsewhere; consider a shared dependency module so future routers don't duplicate auth code.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on M4 pages (all required data-testids present, KPIs populated, journal entry from backend test visible, subpoena detail modal renders audit trail + response record)

test_credentials: admin@notarychain.com / Admin123! · notarytest@test.com / Test123! · demo@test.com / Demo123!
seed_data_creation: Created via pytest: 1 journal entry (TEST_cer_m4_01, acknowledgment, TEST Jane Doe, $25), 1 subpoena (TEST-CASE-M4-001) now in 'responded' status with audit_log = [intake, exported, responded]. Safe TEST_ prefix.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://notary-fl-m3.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/test_fl_journal_m4.py -v — repeatable. For UI testing use the token-injection pattern (login via fetch to /api/auth/login then localStorage.setItem('access_token', token)) rather than form submit, until the LoginPage redirect timing is debugged. M3 readiness endpoint smoke-checked (200/404 not 500).
rca of the issue: No new bugs introduced by M4. The pre-existing UI login form non-redirect is the only observation — backend /api/auth/login returns {access_token, token_type, requires_2fa:false, temp_token:null}; the field expected by frontend is 'access_token' (confirmed by handoff note) so the redirect should fire. Likely a small race/promise-handling thing in LoginPage; doesn't block any M4 functionality once authenticated via any path.
